title: Progress
whatItIs:
Progress is the idea that some changes make life meaningfully better: less death, less suffering, more capability, more freedom, or more choice. It can happen through science, policy, technology, or social reform.

The topic also asks what counts as improvement, because not every change that grows capacity is good in the ways people care about.
whyItMatters:
It matters because almost every political and technological debate hides a claim about progress. People disagree not only on whether something works, but on whether it improves human life.

A good model of progress helps you ask better questions about tradeoffs, distribution, and unintended consequences.
frontier: The frontier is whether AI, biotech, and energy systems can unlock progress faster than they create new instability. That is a civilizational question, not just a technical one.
openQuestions: Can progress be measured without flattening values?
